domains.purpose: Defines the data models used throughout the application, encapsulating the structure and behavior of the data.
domains.high_level_architecture: The models are structured to interact with the database and provide a clear interface for data manipulation.
database_schema_notes: Data model corresponds to the 'data' table in the database.
Fields include id, name, value, created_at, updated_at.
testing_strategy: Unit tests for each model method.
Integration tests for data fetching and manipulation.
operational_considerations: Ensure data integrity during CRUD operations.

domains.purpose: Contains the various modules of the application, each responsible for a specific feature or functionality.
domains.high_level_architecture: Modules are organized by feature, with controllers managing the business logic and views handling the UI.

api_contracts.description: Retrieves details of a specific task.

database_schema_notes: Modules interact with the 'tasks' table for task-related operations.
Ensure foreign key relationships are maintained.
external_integrations: Integration with notification service for task reminders.
testing_strategy: Unit tests for controllers and views.
operational_considerations: Monitor performance of task loading and rendering.

domains.purpose: Provides utility functions and classes that assist with common tasks and operations throughout the application.
domains.high_level_architecture: Utilities are stateless functions that can be reused across different modules and components.
testing_strategy: Unit tests for each utility function.
operational_considerations: Document usage and edge cases for each utility.

domains.purpose: Contains the version 3 of the application, focusing on new features and improvements over previous versions.
domains.high_level_architecture: Versioned architecture allows for backward compatibility while introducing new functionalities.
database_schema_notes: New fields may be added to support version 3 features.
Migration scripts should be created for database upgrades.
testing_strategy: Regression tests to ensure previous functionality is not broken.
operational_considerations: Monitor for issues related to version compatibility.
Provide clear migration paths for users upgrading from previous versions.
